title: Semantik Architect Frame Schema — Entity: Artifact
description: JSON schema for the entity-level ArtifactFrame. This frame models individual physical artifacts such as buildings, monuments, artworks, tools, vehicles-as-objects, inscriptions, engineered structures, and similar discrete objects for encyclopedic lead sentences and short summaries.

frame_type.description: Frame family identifier. The canonical value is "entity.artifact". The shorter legacy alias "artifact" is accepted for compatibility with older pipelines and frame routers.

id.description: Optional stable identifier for this frame instance (for example an internal frame ID, page ID, or statement-cluster ID). This is not the same as the artifact entity's own identifier.

main_entity.description: The artifact being described. This is the discourse subject that article text or summaries are about.

entity_role.description: Optional role label describing how main_entity functions in this frame. For article-level entity frames this is usually "subject".

artifact_type_lemmas.description: Lemma-like labels describing the artifact's coarse classification, such as ["painting"], ["cathedral"], ["statue"], ["bridge"], or ["granodiorite stele"].

function_lemmas.description: Short lemma-like phrases describing the artifact's primary use or role, such as ["railway station"], ["suspension bridge"], or ["military fortress"].

makers.description: Entities primarily responsible for creating, designing, constructing, or manufacturing the artifact.

owners.description: Current or historically notable owners, custodians, or holding institutions.

current_location.description: Where the artifact is currently located, displayed, stored, or installed. This may be modeled as a generic entity or a dedicated location reference.

creation_event.description: Optional explicit event representing creation, construction, commissioning, excavation, or another origin event for the artifact.

production_timespan.description: Time span over which the artifact was created, produced, built, or otherwise brought into existence.

materials_lemmas.description: Primary materials used in the artifact, expressed as lemma-like strings such as ["granodiorite"], ["marble"], ["steel"], or ["concrete"].

heritage_designations.description: Important heritage, legal-protection, preservation, or collection-status labels associated with the artifact.

usage_notes.description: Short free-form description of notable use, status, condition, or context, such as "still in active use", "now a museum object", or "partially ruined".

dimensions.description: List of physical dimensions associated with the artifact, such as height, length, width, depth, span, diameter, area, or volume.

weight_kg.description: Approximate overall weight of the artifact in kilograms, when notable and reliably sourced.

technical_specs.description: Optional structured technical specification dictionary for artifact-specific parameters such as capacity, span count, engine power, or material grade.

attributes.description: Additional attribute-style facts not modeled as dedicated top-level fields but useful for normalization or surface realization, for example designer, architectural_style, movement, collection, inventory_numbers, inscriptions, or condition_status.

key_events.description: Notable events directly connected to the artifact that may matter for lead sentences or summaries, such as discovery, restoration, relocation, damage, preservation, or unveiling.

extra.description: Opaque metadata carried through normalization for traceability, debugging, or source preservation. It is not part of the core semantic contract for generation.

EntityRef.description: Lightweight reference to a discourse entity such as a person, organization, place, institution, work, class, or generic named thing.

id.description: Stable identifier for the entity, such as a Wikidata QID, URI, or local database ID.

name.description: Preferred surface label for the entity.

gender.description: Optional coarse gender category for human entities.

entity_type.description: Optional coarse type hint such as person, organization, place, building, artwork, artifact, or museum.

lemmas.description: Optional lemma-like strings associated with the entity for lexical realization or classification.

features.description: Optional feature map for language-independent or morphosyntactic attributes.

extra.description: Opaque metadata for IDs, source rows, aliases, or provenance.

LocationRef.description: Reference to a location such as a city, site, museum, building, region, or country.

name.description: Preferred surface label for the location.

kind.description: Optional location kind such as city, country, region, museum, gallery, or archaeological_site.

country_code.description: Optional country code associated with the location.

features.description: Optional feature map for locative behavior, preposition choice, containment, or other location properties.

extra.description: Opaque metadata associated with the location reference.

TimeSpan.description: Coarse time span or single date used for production periods and event times.

start_year.description: Start year. Negative values may be used for BCE dates.

end_year.description: End year. Negative values may be used for BCE dates.

approximate.description: Whether the date or span is approximate.

extra.description: Opaque metadata for original source expressions or normalization details.

EventRef.description: Lightweight reference to an event involving the artifact, such as construction, discovery, relocation, restoration, or unveiling.

event_type.description: Free-form or normalized event type label.

participants.description: Optional mapping from participant roles such as subject, creator, agent, discoverer, owner, or institution to one entity or a list of entities.

Dimension.description: Single named physical dimension associated with the artifact.

kind.description: Dimension label such as height, length, width, depth, span, diameter, area, or volume.

unit.description: Unit for the dimension value, such as m, km, cm, m2, m3, ft, or kg.

extra.description: Optional metadata for this dimension, such as source notes or as_of dates.

examples.usage_notes: Key to modern understanding of Egyptian hieroglyphs.
